title: Resolve Req 4.3 strict-decrease mechanism: scalar violation_count has no prior-pass comparator in evaluate_stop
anchor: task 5.1 _Requirements_ line 102 `_Requirements: 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 10.2, 10.4, 14.1, 14.2_`
change: Req 4.3 (strict-decrease no-progress at spec level → HANDOFF) is listed under stop_hook task 5.1, but evaluate_stop only reads the scalar `run_state.violation_count` and has no access to the PRIOR pass's count to compute strict-decrease (design.md:837-840 describes it as the spec-completion loop's responsibility, NOT the Stop hook's). Either (a) remove 4.3 from task 5.1's _Requirements_ list (the strict-decrease check lives in the spec-completion loop / Initializer, design.md:832-840), OR (b) add a `prev_violation_count` column to the run_state DDL (design.md:481-496) and implement the pass-over-pass comparison inside evaluate_stop. Recommend (a) to keep the Stop hook a pure scalar gate.

Add subtasks under task 5: (5.1b) emit_handoff_summary implementing the design.md:828 content contract; (5.1c) write_run_state/save_run_state persistence; (5.1d) count_items_proven_since / count_commits_since slice-history counters used by check_no_progress.

title: Add Req 13.5 (Property 21 Hook Exit-Code Contract) to stop_hook task 5.1 _Requirements_

Add 13.5 to task 5.1's _Requirements_ (and to task 5.5's unit-test _Requirements at line 128, which currently lists only 10.4) so the Hook Exit-Code Contract is traced to its canonical emitter. This wires the EARS criterion to the component.

title: Capture the N-25 Postgres-unavailable fallback gate-consistency constraints in the Stop-hook load_run_state spec
anchor: task 5.1 line 97 `Implement load_feature_list() and load_run_state() (reading from feature_list.json and claude-progress.txt / in-memory fallback before Postgres)`
change: Note N-25 (tasks.md:730) is a load-bearing obligation on the Stop coverage gate: when db_connection.health_check() fails, the gate MUST fall back to file-backed state (feature_list.json + claude-progress.txt) and reach the SAME allow/block decision it would on Postgres — 'never fail-open', and 'on divergence the gate takes the more conservative (blocking) result.' task 5.1's load_run_state bullet captures only the read order, dropping the three correctness constraints (file-fallback equivalence, never-fail-open, conservative-on-divergence). Add these three constraints to the load_run_state bullet and cross-reference Note N-25.

title: Add a Phase-0 implementation task for the PreToolUse checklist-approval guard (REQ-SPEC-016, Property 27, CHECK-12)

Currently the design hook-table row (design.md:213), Property 27, and CHECK-12 exist but NO build task wires this guard into the hook (task 50 implements the abstract gate logic but does not name the PreToolUse hook check).

title: Fix task 6.4 property-test acceptance to assert the full widened status-edge set
anchor: Task 6.4, line 'assert all are blocked; assert only `status: proven` writes are allowed' (tasks.md:154)
change: Rewrite the acceptance line to: 'assert the permitted status edges {unproven→proven (requires complete Evidence_Record), unproven→failed, failed→unproven} are allowed and all other transitions (identity mutations, into-proven without evidence, any edge outside the set) are blocked.' The current 'only status: proven writes are allowed' would fail a legitimate unproven→failed / failed→unproven write and contradicts requirements.md:123/129, design.md:215, and Property 3 (design.md:900).

title: Define the full ordered 6+-guard chain in task 6.1 and update 'Chain all four checks'
anchor: Task 6.1 'Chain all four checks; return first blocking decision' (tasks.md:136); task 49.1 'chain it BEFORE the existing plan/scope/artifact/status checks' (tasks.md:964)

title: Resolve REQ-AUDIT-001 Phase-0 vs Phase-2 audit-producer wiring split for pre_tool_use_hook.py
anchor: Task 6 (tasks.md:130-137); cross-ref design.md:210 (plan-gate row says it 'calls audit_log.append(...)') and requirements.md:460; producer-wiring lands only in task 52.3 (tasks.md:970)

As written, design.md:210/217 describe a Phase-0 audit-producer behavior that no Phase-0/1 task delivers (audit_log.py and its hook wiring are Phase 2), so REQ-AUDIT-001 is unmet for the whole Phase-0/1 window with no acknowledgement of the deferral.

title: Define the scope-gate's exact command/argument detection patterns

title: Wire REQ-OBS-003 (12.3) gate-decision telemetry as an obligation on pre_tool_use_hook.py
anchor: Task 6 `_Requirements:` line (tasks.md:137); cross-ref task 36.1 which names PreToolUse (tasks.md:539) and requirements.md:236 (12.3)

Currently 12.3 is a binding obligation on this component but task 6 omits OTel emission entirely and 12.3 is not cited anywhere in task 6, leaving the Phase-0/1 PreToolUse hook with no decision telemetry and no acknowledged deferral.

title: Add the schema-validation obligation for feature_list.json writes to task 6
anchor: Task 6.1 check list (tasks.md:131-135); cross-ref design.md:138 ('PreToolUse validates against this') and design.md:623 (Phase-0 spine: 'JSON Schema that PreToolUse validates writes against')
change: Add a `check_schema_validation(event)` step to task 6.1 that JSON-Schema-validates every feature_list.json write against schema/feature_list.schema.json (blocking on a schema-invalid write, e.g. an item with a proven status but a missing output_hash), and cite this in design's status-guard row. title: Wire the Postgres-unavailable file-fallback into the PreToolUse status/scope/integrity guards in task 6
anchor: Task 6.1-6.2 (tasks.md:131-135, file-only reads); cross-ref design.md:815 ('Postgres unavailable | Fall back to feature_list.json...') and the reconciliation note (tasks.md:730)

Cite design.md:815 (not just the general note 730). Currently task 6 reads feature_list.json file-only with no DB path or fallback contract, so the gate-consistency-on-fallback obligation (N-25) is unwired for this hook.

title: Task the SessionStart→state_integrity compute/write (it is currently untasked)
anchor: Task 49 'Resumed-state integrity (REQ-STATE-005)' (tasks.md:963) and its only subtask 49.1; task 7.1 'Implement .claude/hooks/session_start_hook.py' (tasks.md:163-169)
change: design.md:219 and Property 26 (design.md:1088) both state SessionStart 'computes the resumed-state hash via state_integrity.py and writes run_state.resume_integrity_ok', but no task implements this: task 7.1 only does git/progress/coverage load + stdout injection, and task 49/49.1 create state_integrity.py + the PreToolUse guard with NO subtask wiring state_integrity INTO session_start_hook.py. Add a subtask 49.2 (or amend 49) naming the file: 'extend .claude/hooks/session_start_hook.py to import tools/state_integrity.py, call state_integrity.compute_hash(), and write run_state.resume_integrity_ok (Phase-2 extension of the Phase-0 hook; non-blocking, exit 0 always)'. Cross-reference it from task 7 so the Phase-0 component's later mutation is traceable. Extend task 7.2 unit tests with an assertion that resume_integrity_ok is written on resume. _Requirements: 11.3, REQ-STATE-005_.

title: Add resume_integrity_ok (and violation_count, retry_count) to migration task 28.5
anchor: Task 28.5 'Write migration db/migrations/005_run_state.sql' — bullet 'run_state table with status CHECK {running,complete,handoff,blocked}, stop_hook_active boolean, no_progress_n counter'; _Requirements: 11.1, 16.1_ (tasks.md:441-443)
change: The design run_state SQL (design.md:481-496) declares resume_integrity_ok BOOLEAN, violation_count, and retry_count, but task 28.5 lists ONLY status CHECK + stop_hook_active + no_progress_n, so the durable column SessionStart is spec'd to write is un-tasked. Add 'resume_integrity_ok BOOLEAN (REQ-STATE-005; written by SessionStart, read by the PreToolUse integrity guard), violation_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0, retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0' to the column list and add REQ-STATE-005 to the _Requirements:_ line.

title: Make OTel hook.decision span honest for non-gating hooks (SessionStart/PreCompact)
anchor: Task 36.1 'Extend all six hooks ... to emit OTel gate-decision events' — bullet setting attributes hook.event/tool.name/decision(allow/block)/reason/requirement.id on hook.decision span (tasks.md:539-542)
change: Task 36.1 sets a 'decision' (allow/block) attribute uniformly on a 'hook.decision' span for ALL six hooks, but SessionStart and PreCompact never make an allow/block gate decision (always exit 0), so decision=allow/block is degenerate; and requirement.id is ambiguous for SessionStart (tagged with BOTH REQ-STATE-003 and REQ-STATE-005 per design.md:219). Specify the attribute set per hook class: for non-gating hooks (SessionStart, PreCompact) emit an informational span (distinct name e.g. 'hook.session_start' or decision='n/a') and either omit requirement.id or list both ids explicitly, rather than a gate-decision allow/block attribute — so the trace schema reflects which hooks actually gate. (REQ-OBS-003 / criterion 12.3 covers only true gate decisions.)

title: Pin the SessionStart stdout contract so the two phase-0 tests assert the same fidelity
anchor: Task 7.1 'Inject combined summary into Claude Code context via stdout' (tasks.md:167); smoke test 16.1 'stdout contains git/progress/coverage summary' (tasks.md:286); integration test 17.1 'stdout JSON contains git_status, unproven_count, proven_count' (tasks.md:298)
change: Task 7.1 says 'inject combined summary via stdout' without pinning the output shape, so 16.1 asserts a free-text 'contains' summary while 17.1 asserts structured JSON fields (git_status, unproven_count, proven_count) — two different fidelities for the same hook output. Pin the contract in 7.1 (emit structured JSON with named fields git_status, unproven_count, proven_count) and align 16.1 to assert the same JSON fields (or explicitly state 16.1 is the looser smoke check by design), so the output contract is single-sourced.

title: Cover where SessionStart writes resume_integrity_ok when Postgres is unavailable (N-25)
anchor: Notes bullet N-25 'Postgres-unavailable fallback gate-consistency' (tasks.md:730), which enumerates 'Stop coverage gate, PreToolUse status/integrity guards, OPA merge policy' but not SessionStart's write of resume_integrity_ok
change: N-25 mandates file-backed fallback for the read-side gates but is silent on SessionStart's WRITE of run_state.resume_integrity_ok (a Postgres run_state column per design.md:492) when health_check() fails at session start. If Postgres is down at SessionStart, the spec does not say whether the flag is written to the claude-progress.txt fallback or skipped — yet the downstream PreToolUse integrity guard reads it. Extend N-25 to state where SessionStart records resume_integrity_ok under DB-unavailable (write to the file-backed run_state duplicate, reconcile on reconnect), keeping the conservative/blocking behavior.

title: Specify audit_log.append is called inside the decision path before exit (ordering)
anchor: Task 52.3 'Wire audit-log producer into the three hooks — modify stop_hook.py, pre_tool_use_hook.py, and subagent_stop_hook.py to call tools/audit_log.append(...) on every allow/block decision' (tasks.md:970); and the Postgres-fallback note (tasks.md:730)
change: Specify in task 52.3 (and the new design subsection) that for subagent_stop_hook.py the audit_log.append call is invoked INSIDE the decision path BEFORE the exit, wrapped so an append failure degrades to the file-backed log (per the tasks.md:730 fallback) and NEVER suppresses or skips the gate decision/exit. Add a unit test: 'audit_log.append raises (e.g. Postgres down before file-fallback engages) -> the allow/block decision is still recorded to the file-backed log AND the hook exits with the correct code'. Without this, a fail-closed unhandled-exception exit 2 (task 8.1 / tasks.md:182) could fire before any audit entry is written, breaking REQ-AUDIT-001's 'on every allow/block decision' obligation (requirements.md:460).

title: Fix non-canonical field name acting_agent in Property 24 PBT (task 39.13)
anchor: Task 39.13 'Generate evidence chains with arbitrary `acting_agent` identities; assert any Evidence_Record where `acting_agent == "implementer.md"` is rejected' (tasks.md:655)
change: Rename `acting_agent` to `actor_agent` in task 39.13 (both occurrences). design.md:473 declares 'Canonical name is actor_agent everywhere (no acting_agent)' and Property 24 (design.md:1068) uses `actor_agent`, but the validating PBT in tasks.md:655 still generates and rejects on the non-canonical `acting_agent` key — so the role-separation test would read the wrong field and fail to enforce the gate. This is a live naming-drift defect.

title: Map REQ-STEER-004 (command-type fail-closed) as an explicit obligation on this hook
anchor: Task 9.1 registers SubagentStop as "type":"command" (tasks.md:193-194); task 9.2 smoke test asserts all hooks command-type (tasks.md:197-199); requirement 13.4/REQ-STEER-004 (requirements.md:253)
change: Wire REQ-STEER-004 (command-type hooks fail closed; no HTTP/MCP for hard policy, requirements.md:253) explicitly onto subagent_stop_hook.py: add the requirement tag to the SubagentStop registration (task 9.1) and reference the task-9.2 smoke test (tests/smoke/test_hook_config.py) which parses settings.json and asserts EVERY registered hook including SubagentStop is command-type with no http/mcp. This is the reason the hook can fail closed (exit 2) at all; currently it is only an un-tagged behavior note.

title: Single out SubagentStop in the Property 21 exit-code-contract test
anchor: Task 39.4 'Write property test for hook exit-code contract' / test_hook_exit_code_contract, generic over hooks (tasks.md:593-597); Property 21 (design.md:1042-1046); requirement 13.5 (requirements.md:254)
change: Extend task 39.4 (Property 21, Hook Exit-Code Contract) to explicitly include a SubagentStop case: assert the hook's allow(exit 0)/block(exit 2 with stderr)/fail-closed-on-exception(exit 2) behavior conforms to REQ-STEER-005 (requirements.md:254). The exit-code contract is the foundation of this hook's entire allow/block behavior, yet the validating test (test_hook_exit_code_contract) is generic across hooks and does not single out SubagentStop, leaving its exit-code conformance only transitively covered.

title: REQ-OBS-003 gate-decision forwarding mis-fits PostToolUse (it makes no allow/block decision)
anchor: Task 36.1 (tasks.md:539-542): "Extend all six hooks (Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SubagentStop, SessionStart, PreCompact) to emit OTel gate-decision events" with attribute `decision` (allow/block)
change: PostToolUse never makes an allow/block gate decision (it always exits 1, non-blocking), but task 36.1 sets a `decision`(allow/block) attribute on its span for 'all six hooks' and REQ-OBS-003 (requirements.md:236) is triggered only 'WHEN a hook makes a gate decision'. Fix: in task 36.1 (and the design narrative) emit a distinct span event for PostToolUse — e.g. span name 'hook.feedback' with attributes `hook.event`, `tool.name`, `findings_count` (and severity breakdown) — instead of reusing the allow/block `decision` attribute that is meaningless for a non-gating hook. Clarify that REQ-OBS-003's gate-decision forwarding applies to the five gate-making hooks; PostToolUse forwards informational feedback telemetry, not an audit-logged gate decision.

title: Reconcile the checkpointed artifact set across the three specs (and remove the orphan snapshot)
anchor: tasks.md:392-394 (task 25.1 bullets: append claude-progress.txt; write claude-progress-evidence-snapshot.json; `git add -f ... && git commit`) vs design.md:220 (PreCompact row 'Checkpoints claude-progress.txt, current evidence state, and feature_list.json') vs requirements.md:219-220
change: Make the file list identical in tasks.md:392-394 and design.md:220, and ensure every written file has a reader. As written, tasks.md commits claude-progress.txt + claude-progress-evidence-snapshot.json but NOT feature_list.json, while design.md:220 commits feature_list.json and never names the snapshot; the snapshot filename appears exactly once in the whole spec (tasks.md:393 write) and is read by nothing — SessionStart (tasks.md:165-166) reads only claude-progress.txt + feature_list.json. Recommended fix: drop claude-progress-evidence-snapshot.json from tasks.md:393 and instead commit feature_list.json directly (so SessionStart re-orients from it per design.md:219); add feature_list.json to the `git add`/commit at tasks.md:394; set design.md:220 to the same set {claude-progress.txt, feature_list.json}. If the derived snapshot is kept, add it to design.md:220 AND give it a reader in session_start_hook.py (task 7.1).

title: Fix settings.json owning-file path drift and stale task cross-reference for PreCompact binding
anchor: tasks.md:16 `Create `.vscode/settings.json` (or `.kiro/settings.json`) stub for hook wiring (populated in task 8)`
change: Two defects on this line: (1) 'populated in task 8' is wrong — task 8 is subagent_stop_hook.py (tasks.md:176); the hook-wiring task that registers PreCompact is task 9 (tasks.md:191-193). Change to 'populated in task 9'. (2) The stub path `.vscode/settings.json` is both inconsistent with task 9.1 / design.md:157 (`.claude/settings.json`) AND actively gitignored (.gitignore:32 ignores `.vscode/`), so a PreCompact binding placed there would never be committed and the runtime would never load it (silent fail-open wiring hole). Standardize the path to `.claude/settings.json` at tasks.md:16. The actual PreCompact command binding `python3 .claude/hooks/pre_compact_hook.py` is specified at design.md:193-198 and registered by task 9.1.

title: Drop misleading `git add -f` on non-gitignored checkpoint files (or add the intended .gitignore entry)
anchor: tasks.md:394 `Commit both files via `git add -f ... && git commit -m "chore: PreCompact checkpoint [skip ci]"` if anything has changed`
change: The `-f` (force-add) implies claude-progress.txt / the snapshot are gitignored, but the actual .gitignore (lines 1-47) ignores only __pycache__, venvs, dist/build, test/coverage caches, .idea//.vscode/, OS files, .playwright-mcp/, and .env* — there is NO claude-progress entry. Resolve the intent mismatch: either (a) drop the `-f` so it reads `git add claude-progress.txt feature_list.json` (the files are tracked normally), or (b) if checkpoint files are meant to be ignored, add them to .gitignore and make task 1 (tasks.md:17 'Create ... .gitignore entries') actually create that entry — which justifies the `-f`. Recommend (a) given SessionStart must read feature_list.json back.

title: PreCompact wrongly included in gate-decision OTel forwarding (makes no allow/block decision)
anchor: tasks.md:539-541 task 36.1 `Extend all six hooks (Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SubagentStop, SessionStart, PreCompact) to emit OTel gate-decision events ... attributes: hook.event, tool.name, decision (allow/block), reason, requirement.id`
change: PreCompact makes no gate decision — it always exits 0, has no allow/block, no requirement.id, and the design.md:220 PreCompact row explicitly lists 'Nothing (checkpoint write; non-blocking)' with no audit_log.append (unlike Stop:209, PreToolUse:210, SubagentStop:217-218 which DO call audit_log.append). REQ-OBS-003 (requirements.md:236) scopes the event to 'WHEN a hook makes a gate decision'. Fix task 36.1: either (a) exclude PreCompact from gate-decision forwarding and emit a distinct non-gate span for it (e.g. `hook.checkpoint` with item-count attributes, no decision/requirement.id), or (b) state that for PreCompact the `decision` attribute is a fixed checkpoint marker (not allow/block) and requirement.id is omitted. Update the ears_story for Requirement 12.3 accordingly.

title: Relabel the REQ-STATE-001 ears-story's 11.4 reference (per-slice commit, not PreCompact)
anchor: tasks.md:18 task 1 `_Requirements: 11.1, 11.4_`
change: When mapping PreCompact's contribution to durable state, do NOT cite criterion 11.4 as a PreCompact/claude-progress.txt anchor: 11.4 (requirements.md:222) is 'record an incremental commit per completed Slice as the durable execution trail' — owned by the implementer's per-slice commit, NOT by PreCompact. PreCompact's genuine REQ-STATE anchors are 11.1 (persist outside model context) and 11.2 (the checkpoint itself). Keep task 1's tag `11.1, 11.4` as-is (task 1 legitimately scaffolds claude-progress.txt for 11.1 and the git/.gitignore trail relevant to 11.4), but in any PreCompact coverage note label the 11.4 link as 'task 1's scaffolding tag, unrelated to PreCompact' so no false PreCompact->11.4 coverage edge is implied.

title: collect() must emit timezone-aware ISO-8601, not datetime.utcnow()
anchor: Task 3.1, tasks.md:45 — 'capture ISO-8601 `collected_at` from `datetime.utcnow()`'
change: Change the collect() spec at tasks.md:45 from `datetime.utcnow()` to `datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()` (timezone-aware, emits a '+00:00' offset). Rationale to add inline: the EvidenceRecord schema requires `format: date-time` (RFC 3339) at design.md:407 and the `evidence_records.collected_at` column is `TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL` (design.md:472) with an `evidence_complete` CHECK requiring `collected_at IS NOT NULL` (design.md:476). A naive utcnow() string has no offset, can fail strict date-time validation, and would be coerced to a different instant on Postgres insert — breaking Property 19's 'all four fields shall match the original capture values without loss' (design.md:1028). Mirror the same correction in the unit-test task 3.3 (tasks.md:56-61) so a test asserts the emitted timestamp carries an offset.

title: validate_evidence_record() must validate output_hash/collected_at formats, not just presence
anchor: Task 3.1, tasks.md:46 — 'validate_evidence_record(record: dict) -> bool that checks all four fields present and non-empty'
change: Strengthen validate_evidence_record() at tasks.md:46 to validate `output_hash` against the anchored pattern `^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$` and `collected_at` as a parseable ISO-8601/RFC-3339 timestamp (or run jsonschema against the EvidenceRecord definition, design.md:387-411), not merely presence+non-emptiness. Reason: the SubagentStop hook calls this same validator (tasks.md:179), so a non-empty but malformed output_hash (uppercase hex, missing `sha256:` prefix, wrong length) currently passes both the validator and the SubagentStop gate (only the separate JSON-Schema path in the PreToolUse status guard catches it). This also closes the requirements.md:521 deferral rationale, which justifies skipping Pydantic/BAML runtime enforcement by asserting 'the SubagentStop schema-validation gate (REQ-COV-006) already enforces Evidence_Record structure at the hook layer' — a claim that is false while tasks.md:46/179 wire the hook to a presence-only validator. Cite design.md:402 (canonical sha256 pattern).

title: store_to_feature_list() and PreToolUse status-guard task text must admit the failed-state transitions
anchor: Task 3.1, tasks.md:47 ('flips status to `proven`') and task 6.1, tasks.md:135 ('validate proposed transition is `unproven → proven` only')
change: Update tasks.md:47 and tasks.md:135 to match the 2026-06-15 reconciliation that widened permitted transitions. tasks.md:135 currently says 'validate proposed transition is `unproven → proven` only' — change to: permit {unproven→proven, unproven→failed, failed→unproven}; only any transition *into* proven additionally requires a complete Evidence_Record (unproven→failed and failed→unproven require none). Align tasks.md:47's store_to_feature_list() description to the same edge set. This reconciles the stale task text with requirements.md:123,129, design.md:215 (PreToolUse status guard) and design.md:372,374 (status enum {unproven,proven,failed}) and Property 3 (design.md:900).

title: Property 19 over-claims Postgres round-trip while filed as a Phase-0 collector subtask
anchor: Task 3.2, tasks.md:50-54 — '**Property 19: Evidence Round-Trip (Store and Retrieve)** ... Validates: Requirements 16.2'
change: Property 19 (design.md:1026-1030) asserts the record is 'stored in Postgres ... retrievable by requirement_id and commit_sha', but task 3.2 is a Phase-0 collector subtask with NO Postgres (store_to_postgres is Phase-2 task 30.1, tasks.md:476). Split the property: keep a Phase-0 in-memory/file round-trip (collect→validate→hash-match, no Postgres) under task 3.2 and validate against 5.3/5.6 not 16.2; add the Phase-2 Postgres store/retrieve round-trip assertion under task 30.2 (tasks.md:480) validating 16.2. Reconcile the 'stored in Postgres' wording in the Phase-0 portion of Property 19 at design.md:1028 accordingly.

title: actor_agent: add as a store_to_postgres parameter (NOT to the 4-field JSON record) and fix stale acting_agent name
anchor: Task 30.1, tasks.md:477 ('store_to_postgres(record: EvidenceRecord, req_id: str, commit_sha: str)') and Property 24 test, tasks.md:655 ('acting_agent == "implementer.md"')
change: Property 24 requires actor_agent (= verifier.md, never implementer.md) on every evidence row (design.md:473,1068), but store_to_postgres()'s signature (tasks.md:477) carries no verifier identity, so the role-separation invariant has no enforcement point at the collector boundary. Add `actor_agent` as a parameter: `store_to_postgres(record, req_id, commit_sha, actor_agent)`, asserting it equals 'verifier.md' and rejecting implementer-authored evidence at write time. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: do NOT add actor_agent to the EvidenceRecord JSON object — that schema is `additionalProperties: false` over exactly the four fields (design.md:390; requirements.md:29), so adding a fifth field would break Property 2/CHECK-7 and be rejected by the SubagentStop and PreToolUse status-guard validators. actor_agent lives only on the 7-column evidence_records Postgres row (design.md:464-478). Separately fix the stale name at tasks.md:655: the Property 24 test still references `acting_agent`, but design.md:473 declares 'Canonical name is actor_agent everywhere (no acting_agent)' — rename `acting_agent` → `actor_agent` in the task-655 test description.

title: Decide empty/zero-byte artifact validity in collect()
anchor: Task 3.3, tasks.md:57 — 'collect() with empty bytes still produces valid sha256 hash'
change: tasks.md:57 asserts empty bytes 'still produces valid sha256 hash' (sha256('') = e3b0c442...), but the spec never decides whether a zero-byte artifact is a VALID proof — such a hash is well-formed and passes all four-field and schema checks, so the gate would silently accept an empty/short-circuited test as proof. Decide and specify one of: (a) collect() raises on empty output_bytes; or (b) the Verifier must assert the artifact is non-trivial before calling collect(). Then make tasks.md:57's test assert the chosen behavior (reject-empty, or document why empty is acceptable) rather than blessing the meaningless hash. Add a matching EARS criterion or note under Requirement 5.3 / 9.3 if option (a) changes the collector contract.

title: Wire REQ-EXEC-012 / 8.3 WIRING integration-test evidence into the collector scope
anchor: Task 3.1, tasks.md:44-48 (_Requirements: 5.3, 5.6, 9.3_) and task 47, tasks.md:959 (WIRING integration-test evidence)
change: Requirement 8.3 (requirements.md:174: 'WHEN a WIRING item is claimed proven, THE System SHALL require an integration test exercising the real execution path as the attached Evidence_Record') and the schema note 'WIRING items require integration-test evidence' (design.md:334) bear directly on evidence_collector.py — it is the assembler of that integration-test Evidence_Record. Task 47 (tasks.md:959) names the obligation but the collector task 3.1 only frames output_bytes around behavioral/Playwright output. Add 8.3 to task 3.1's `_Requirements:` line and a sub-point that collect() must accept and assemble an integration-test artifact (not only a behavioral/Playwright one), so the WIRING-proof path has an explicit assembly point.

title: Wire REQ-VERIFY-007/008 (Req 25.1/25.2) NFR Evidence_Record assembly into the collector
anchor: Task 3.1, tasks.md:44-48 (_Requirements: 5.3, 5.6, 9.3_) and task 51, tasks.md:966 (perf_a11y_verifier emits NFR Evidence_Records)
change: Requirements 25.1/25.2 (requirements.md:447-448) require performance verification to 'attach the result as the Evidence_Record' (k6/Lighthouse) and accessibility verification (axe-core) to produce evidence; task 51 (tasks.md:966) emits these as 'NFR Evidence_Records.' These records are assembled by evidence_collector.py, but task 3.1 frames collect()/output_bytes only around behavioral test output. Add REQ-VERIFY-007/008 to the collector's bearing-requirements and a note that output_bytes for NFR items is the k6/Lighthouse/axe-core result artifact — which makes resolving the byte-source question (dedup evidence-hash-byte-source) mandatory for non-test artifacts.

title: store_to_postgres fallback must be flagged as an N-25 gate-consistency obligation (never fail-open)
anchor: Task 30.1, tasks.md:477 ('fall back to feature_list.json update if DB unavailable') and task 30.2, tasks.md:482
change: store_to_postgres's feature_list.json fallback (tasks.md:477,482) is specified only mechanically. Per the N-25 reconciliation obligation (tasks.md:730), when db_connection.health_check() fails the file-backed fallback MUST reach the SAME allow/block decision as Postgres and NEVER fail-open, taking the more conservative (blocking) result on divergence. Add this gate-consistency constraint to task 30.1/30.2 and cite REQ-STORE-001/003 (requirements.md:296,298 — Postgres single source of truth, reconstructable independently of any model session). A fail-open or divergent collector fallback would silently weaken the OPA zero-evidence gate.

title: Add evidence-quality caveat: no Z3 check exercises collect()'s real sha256/datetime/format behavior
anchor: Task 3.3, tasks.md:56-61 (unit tests for evidence_collector.py) and the CHECK-7 family in verification/formal_verification_merged.py:301-338
change: CHECK-7a/7b/7c (formal_verification_merged.py:311-338) operate purely on abstract booleans (`evidenceComplete == And(evidenceHasHash, evidenceHasTestFile, ...)`, line 302) — they model the four-field GATE LOGIC, not the collector's hashing/timestamp behavior. NO Z3 check exercises collect()'s actual sha256 computation, datetime output, or the `^sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$` format; those are only unit-tested (task 3.3, tasks.md:57-59). Add an explicit caveat to task 3.3 (or the testing-strategy note) stating that the sha256/timestamp/format correctness is unit-test-verified only and is NOT in the Z3 coverage, so the Z3 'evidence schema enforced' claim is not mistaken for runtime hash-format coverage.

title: No ingestion path turns the WIRING candidate into a feature_list.json coverage item (8.1 second clause)
anchor: Task 19.1 body, tasks.md:329 "For each dead-code finding, emit a WIRING coverage item candidate (JSON to stdout)" — the candidate stops at stdout
change: Add a subtask (e.g. 19.3) OR extend post_tool_use_hook.py task 18.1 specifying the ingestion path that satisfies criterion 8.1's second clause ('SHALL represent each wiring obligation as a WIRING coverage item in feature_list.json'). State the actor and the write path: the implementer/initializer, on receiving the wiring_checker.py candidate as PostToolUse next-turn feedback, MUST add a type:'WIRING' CoverageItem (status default 'unproven', in_scope true, ≥1 acceptance criterion per Property 1) to feature_list.json via the PreToolUse status-guard-permitted creation path (design.md:215). Cite REQ-EXEC-010. Today the data path stops at stdout/next-turn text and no task creates the item.

title: Division of labor between wiring_checker.py (AST) and wiring_dead_code.yml (Semgrep, task 24.1) is unresolved
anchor: Task 19.1 body tasks.md:328 (AST 'never called' detection) vs task 24.1 tasks.md:385-386 (Semgrep route/handler decorators + registered-but-uninvoked callbacks); both carry _Requirements: 8.1, 8.2_
change: Add a one-line division-of-labor note (in task 19.1 and cross-referenced in 24.1): 'wiring_checker.py (AST) covers plain defined-but-never-called functions; wiring_dead_code.yml (Semgrep) covers decorator-routed handlers/routes and registered-but-uninvoked callbacks; both emit type:WIRING candidates, de-duplicated by symbol before ingestion.' This resolves which tool authoritatively detects the route/handler/callback obligations named in criterion 8.2 and prevents double-reporting. Currently both tools claim the same 8.1/8.2 scope with no de-dup or boundary stated.

title: Changed-files input contract between post_tool_use_hook.py and wiring_checker.py is unspecified (incl. MultiEdit)
anchor: Task 18.1 tasks.md:310 'Triggered on Write, Edit, MultiEdit completion' / tasks.md:313 'Run python3 tools/wiring_checker.py on changed files'; and task 19.1 tasks.md:326 'Accept a list of changed files as CLI args'
change: Specify the handoff contract on both sides: in task 18.1 state how the PostToolUse hook derives the changed-file list from the hook payload (e.g. tool_input.file_path for Write/Edit; the set of edited paths for MultiEdit, since one MultiEdit call can mutate multiple files) and passes them as argv; in task 19.1 state the exact argv format (one file path per positional arg). The matcher is Write|Edit|MultiEdit (design.md:178,216) so the MultiEdit multi-file case must be handled. Today neither side pins the format.

title: Wire orphan_detector.py as a blocking traceability gate to satisfy REQ-6.3 'block the run'
anchor: Task 20.1 'Implement tools/orphan_detector.py' (tasks.md:339-344), _Requirements: 6.3; and task 14.2 required-checks list (tasks.md:270)
change: Add a blocking caller for orphan_detector. Create a new task (e.g. 20.x / 40.x) 'traceability-gate CI workflow': add `.github/workflows/traceability-gate.yml` that runs `python3 tools/orphan_detector.py` and fails the merge (non-zero exit) when any orphan is found; register `traceability-gate` as a REQUIRED status check. Add `traceability-gate` to the required-checks list at tasks.md:270 (`docs/github-ruleset.md`) alongside formal-verification/coverage-gate/secrets-scan/audit-chain-verify. State the exit-code-to-block contract (CI required check fails merge; equivalently a PreToolUse exit-2 gate). This is the production mechanism the REQ-6.3 'block the run' obligation currently lacks — orphan_detector's non-zero exit is consumed by nothing but a test (task 26.1).

title: Define the authoritative store for the backward-orphan 'maps to no verification' lookup
anchor: Task 20.1, bullets at tasks.md:341-342 ('Read feature_list.json for all known requirement IDs' / 'requirement IDs with no source file or verification artifact')
change: Specify the authoritative source for the backward-orphan verification-artifact link. Postgres is the declared single source of truth for coverage queries (requirements.md:298) and the requirement<->evidence graph lives in traceability_links + evidence_records (design.md:453-478), yet task 20.1 reads feature_list.json (tasks.md:341), which can diverge. Add a bullet: query evidence_records / traceability_links (link_type IN ('test','evidence')) for the verification-artifact lookup, with a documented feature_list.json fallback consistent with the Postgres-unavailable fallback rule (design.md:815, tasks.md:730 N-25). Reconcile with REQ-16.3.

title: Define 'verification artifact' concretely for the backward-orphan check
anchor: Task 20.1 bullet (tasks.md:342) and Property 11 (design.md:962-966)
change: Define what counts as a 'verification artifact' so the backward-orphan pass/fail boundary is implementable. The Postgres schema already provides the exact candidate definition (design.md:457-458 fixes traceability_links.link_type to the closed set including 'test'/'evidence'; design.md:465-477 defines evidence_records) but it is never bound to task 20's rule. Add to task 20.1 AND Property 11: a backward orphan = a requirement ID with NO traceability_links row of link_type IN ('test','evidence') AND/OR no evidence_records row. State this is the canonical definition the check keys on.

title: Pin orphan_detector run scope, file glob, language scope, and excludes
anchor: Task 20.1 bullet (tasks.md:340) 'Scan all source files...'
change: Resolve scan-scope ambiguity. tasks.md:340 says 'all source files' (full-repo, CI-suited) while the sibling wiring_checker/PostToolUse pipeline operates on changed files (tasks.md:313,326). Add bullets to task 20.1: (1) state full-repo scope (recommended, since it is wired as a CI blocking gate per the traceability-gate edit); (2) define the file glob and exclude list (e.g. exclude tests/, generated dirs, __init__.py, migrations); (3) state the language scope (Python source). This determines where it can be wired and bounds false positives.

title: Add an orphan-exemption / allowlist mechanism to bound forward-orphan false positives
anchor: Task 20.1 bullet (tasks.md:342) 'source files/functions with no requirement ref (forward orphans)' and Property 11 (design.md:962-966)
change: Add an exemption mechanism so the forward-orphan rule does not fail-noisy on legitimately un-annotated units (helpers, __init__.py, config, generated code). Add to task 20.1 and Property 11: an explicit ignore mechanism — a `# orphan-exempt: <reason>` inline marker AND/OR a config file of excluded paths — and define granularity (file-level vs function-level). Without this the check is unimplementable as a non-noisy blocking gate.

title: Factor commit-trailer ID extraction into one shared helper and state ownership
anchor: Task 20.1 bullet (tasks.md:340, commit-trailer scan) and task 29.1 (tasks.md:464-466, parse_commit_trailers / assert_commit_has_req_id)
change: Two tools implement the same `[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}` commit-trailer scan with no division of labor: orphan_detector (tasks.md:340) and traceability_writer.parse_commit_trailers/assert_commit_has_req_id (tasks.md:464,466). Add a bullet factoring trailer-ID extraction into one shared helper (e.g. tools/req_id_scan.py) imported by both, and state authority: traceability_writer.assert_commit_has_req_id owns the commit-has-req-id gate (REQ-6.2 / Property 10); orphan_detector owns bidirectional orphan detection (REQ-6.3 / Property 11). Prevents regex drift / double-maintenance.

title: Fix task 26.1 _Requirements mapping to include 6.3 (the only task that runs orphan_detector)
anchor: Task 26.1 _Requirements line (tasks.md:410): '_Requirements: 8.1, 8.2, 9.4, 10.3, 13.2_'
change: Add `6.3` to the _Requirements line of task 26.1. Task 26.1 (tasks.md:404-410) is the ONLY task whose body actually runs orphan_detector.py ('run orphan_detector.py; assert exit non-zero and report contains both orphan types', tasks.md:406) yet its mapping omits 6.3. Conversely task 33.1 lists 6.3 (tasks.md:507) but its body never invokes orphan_detector — it tests parse_commit_trailers/migrations/evidence/run-state only. The spec's own forward 6.3->task link points at the wrong task. Add 6.3 to 26.1; optionally drop 6.3 from 33.1's mapping (or keep for parse_commit_trailers' 6.x coverage but note 26.1 owns the orphan-detection assertion).

title: Wire a blocking enforcer for REQ-6.2 commit-trailer obligation (shares orphan_detector's unwired-gate defect)
anchor: Task 29.1 (tasks.md:466) 'assert_commit_has_req_id(commit_sha): raise TraceabilityError if no requirement ID found in trailer'; REQ-6.2 (requirements.md:142)
change: REQ-6.2 ('WHEN a commit is created, require >=1 referenced requirement ID in the commit trailer', requirements.md:142) has no blocking enforcer wired: assert_commit_has_req_id (tasks.md:466) only raises TraceabilityError with no stated caller/hook/CI gate. Both 6.2 and 6.3 share the same unwired-enforcement defect. Add a bullet naming the caller for assert_commit_has_req_id — fold it into the same traceability-gate CI check (or a commit-msg/PreToolUse hook) so a single shared commit-trailer gate satisfies both REQ-6.2 and REQ-6.3's trailer obligation.

title: Disambiguate the requirement-ID regex for multi-segment IDs in the orphan scan
anchor: Task 20.1 bullet (tasks.md:340) regex `[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}`; CoverageItem.id pattern `^[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}$` (design.md:328)
change: Clarify the ID regex's match scope. The detector scans for unanchored `[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}` (tasks.md:340) and ties it to CoverageItem.id `^[A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}$` (design.md:328), but the actual feature_list.json IDs are multi-segment (REQ-TRACE-001, REQ-VERIFY-007, REQ-SPEC-018; requirements.md:137, design.md:146,148) and the EARS dotted short-IDs (e.g. 6.3) are a different namespace the same regex cannot match. Add a bullet stating the regex matches the canonical 3-segment CoverageItem.id form (unanchored within comments/trailers), explicitly NOT the dotted EARS criterion numbers, and confirm it matches all REQ-<DOMAIN>-NNN IDs present in feature_list.json.

title: Make the Rego deny rule honor in_scope, so it matches the Stop gate
anchor: Task 21.1 — 'policy reads `items[_].status == "unproven"` and denies if any found' (tasks.md:355)
change: Amend task 21.1 (and Property 22 in design.md:1052) so the policy filters `input.items` on `in_scope == true` before applying the status/evidence checks, mirroring evaluate_stop's `in_scope_items = [i for i in feature_list.items if i.in_scope]` (design.md:690) and Requirement 5.7 (requirements.md:128). Without this filter a naive `items[_].status == "unproven"` rule blocks merge on an out-of-scope unproven item, diverging from the Stop gate. State: 'deny rules iterate only over items where in_scope == true.'

title: Close the fail-open hole on `failed` status: deny if status != proven
anchor: Task 21.1 — 'policy reads `items[_].status == "unproven"` and denies if any found' (tasks.md:355)
change: Change the deny rule in task 21.1 (and Property 22) from 'deny if status == "unproven"' to 'deny if status != "proven"' (anything not exactly proven blocks), so a `failed` in-scope item also blocks merge. The status enum is {unproven, proven, failed} (design.md:372) but the policy only references unproven/proven, leaving a `failed` item to silently pass a status==unproven rule. This mirrors the Stop hook's `not_proven = [i for i in in_scope_items if i.status != "proven"]` logic (design.md:731).

title: Enumerate the four Evidence_Record fields in the Rego proven-without-evidence rule
anchor: Task 21.1 — 'Add second rule: deny if any item has `status == "proven"` but `evidence` object is missing or has empty fields' (tasks.md:356)
change: Make task 21.1 Rule 2 assert all four Evidence_Record fields (`test_file`, `test_name`, `output_hash`, `collected_at`) are present and non-empty, mirroring the schema `required` list (design.md:389) and the SubagentStop four-field check (design.md:217, Property 2). Current wording 'evidence object missing or has empty fields' is vague and could pass a proven item carrying a partial 1-field evidence object that the hook layer (Property 2 / Glossary Evidence_Record, requirements.md:29) would reject — an inconsistency between the merge gate and the in-session gate.

title: Fix the forward-reference defect: 'deferred to Phase 1 task 20' should be task 21
anchor: Task 14.1 — 'full Postgres-backed policy deferred to Phase 1 task 20' (tasks.md:265)
change: Change 'deferred to Phase 1 task 20' to 'deferred to Phase 1 task 21' in task 14.1. Task 20 (tasks.md:338) is `orphan_detector.py`, NOT the coverage policy; the full coverage_query.rego policy is task 21 (tasks.md:352). The build chain currently misroutes the component's own forward reference to the wrong task.

title: Add a Phase-1 subtask to re-confirm coverage-gate.yml invokes the swapped policy and stays a required check
anchor: Task 21 — '`coverage_query.rego` — Full OPA Zero-Evidence Merge Policy' (tasks.md:352-358)
change: Add a subtask under task 21 to (a) confirm `.github/workflows/coverage-gate.yml` (created in task 14.1) invokes the Phase-1 full policy after the stub→full swap, and (b) confirm `coverage-gate` remains in the GitHub repository ruleset's required checks (cross-ref task 14.2 at tasks.md:270, whose required-checks list `formal-verification`, `coverage-gate`, `secrets-scan`, `audit-chain-verify` is the only place coverage-gate is named as required). Task 21 currently replaces the .rego content with no task verifying the swapped policy is still the gating one. Also note in task 14.1/21 that the Phase-0 stub (status-only, passes on zero unproven) is intentionally weaker than the Phase-1 full policy (adds evidence presence), so Phase-0 green does NOT imply Phase-1 green — the required-status-check registration must survive the swap (re-register, do not assume continuity).

title: Document that coverage-gate.yml is an overloaded workflow gating three tools, not OPA-only
anchor: Task 14.2 — required status checks list naming `coverage-gate` as a single required check (tasks.md:270); cross-ref tasks.md:264 (OPA), 274 (Playwright), 371 (SonarQube) all in coverage-gate.yml
change: Add a note (in task 14.2 or task 21) that the single required status check `coverage-gate` co-hosts three independent tools in `.github/workflows/coverage-gate.yml`: OPA/Conftest (task 14.1/15... tasks.md:264), Playwright (task 15.1, tasks.md:274), and SonarQube (task 22.1, tasks.md:371). Because all three run under one check name, a Playwright or SonarQube failure blocks merge under the same name, so the OPA policy is NOT independently gating and REQ-GATE-003's 'THE OPA_Conftest required CI status check SHALL run' is satisfied only as a co-tenant. Either (a) document this co-tenancy explicitly so the OPA leg's gating role is traceable, or (b) split the OPA policy into its own job/required check so it gates independently.

title: Wire schema validation into pre_tool_use_hook status guard
anchor: tasks.md:131-136 (task 6.1, pre_tool_use_hook check functions) and design.md:138 ('PreToolUse validates against this')
change: Add a sub-bullet to task 6.1 (tasks.md:131-136): 'load schema/feature_list.schema.json and call jsonschema.validate on the proposed feature_list.json write; block (exit 2) on ValidationError'. design.md:138 claims PreToolUse validates writes against the schema, but the only task that loads the schema is feature_list_init.py (tasks.md:240); no hook task wires jsonschema.validate into the PreToolUse path, so the design.md:138 claim has no implementing code path.

title: Add Req 5.6 to task 6.1 _Requirements to match design.md:215 status-guard mapping
anchor: tasks.md:137 (task 6.1 _Requirements: 5.2, 5.3, 7.4, 7.5, 13.2, 13.3, 13.4, 18.1) vs design.md:215 (status guard enforces REQ-COV-002, REQ-COV-003, REQ-COV-006)
change: Add `5.6` to the _Requirements line of task 6.1 (tasks.md:137). The hook-wiring table at design.md:215 maps the PreToolUse status guard to REQ-COV-002, REQ-COV-003 AND REQ-COV-006, but the building task that implements check_status_transition omits 5.6, so the four-field/non-empty gate (Req 5.6) is mapped to the status guard in design but not traced to it in the task.

title: Add a task implementing the design.md:623 schema negative-validation acceptance
anchor: tasks.md:24-41 (task 2 / 2.1 / 2.2 / 2.3) and design.md:623 (Phase-0 spine row 1 acceptance: 'Schema validates a hand-written valid example; rejects an example missing output_hash')

The smoke/integration tests (task 16.1 tasks.md:281-288, task 17.1) only exercise the four hooks and target subagent_stop_hook's output_hash check — not jsonschema.validate against the schema file — and tasks 2.2/2.3 are optional ([ ]*) hypothesis property tests, so the component's own stated pass condition has no implementing task.

title: Add enum-sync consistency test (JSON schema enums vs Postgres CHECK / spec_validator)
anchor: tasks.md:24-29 (task 2) and design.md:333,347 (JSON type/ears_pattern enums) vs design.md:434,436 (requirements table: type CHECK present, ears_pattern TEXT NOT NULL with NO CHECK)

There is currently no task ensuring these three enum sources stay aligned.

title: requirement_versions column set in tasks.md 28.7 contradicts the authoritative DDL
anchor: 28.7 Write migration `db/migrations/007_requirement_versions.sql` — bullet: "`requirement_versions` table: FK to `requirements`, monotonically increasing `version`, `amended_at`, `amendment_reason`, and a flag/marker for whether the amended item has been re-proven"
change: Rewrite the 28.7 column bullet to mirror the design.md DDL (design.md:1141-1151) verbatim: columns `requirement_id` (FK), `version` INTEGER (monotonically increasing), `prior_text`, `new_text` NOT NULL, `author` NOT NULL, `rationale` NOT NULL, `created_at` TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), `UNIQUE (requirement_id, version)`. Drop the non-canonical names `amended_at`/`amendment_reason` and the standalone 're-proven flag' (re-proof is tracked via resetting `coverage_items.status` to `unproven`, per design.md:1152). Keep the prose 'on amendment the linked coverage item re-enters unproven and COMPLETE is blocked while un-reproven'.

title: actor_agent column missing from task 28.4 evidence_records description
anchor: 28.4 Write migration `db/migrations/004_evidence_records.sql` — bullet: "`evidence_records` table with FK to `requirements`, `evidence_complete` CHECK constraint enforcing no empty fields"
change: Add `actor_agent TEXT NOT NULL` to the 28.4 column list, annotated 'MUST be the Verifier, never the Implementer — Property 24 (design.md:473, 1066-1070)'. title: No migration runner, schema_migrations bookkeeping, or apply-on-deploy step specified
anchor: Task 28 header 'Postgres schema migrations' (tasks.md:423); only application path is the integration tests (28.9, 33.1) on a test Neon branch

title: Specify how file-backed (DB-down) audit entries reconcile their seq on reconnect
anchor: tasks.md:730 `... The \`gate_audit_log\` producer (task 52.1) appends to the file-backed log when the DB is unavailable and reconciles on reconnect.`
change: Under DB-down, the producer appends to the file-backed log and reconciles on reconnect, but seq (BIGSERIAL) does not exist in the file backend and seq is part of the hashed canonical form — so DB-assigned seq on reconnect differs from any file placeholder and re-hashing cannot reproduce the file-time entry_hash; reconciliation is undefined. title: Specify chain-linearization / concurrency control for audit_log.append
anchor: Line 968 (task 52.1): 'Audit-log PRODUCER tools/audit_log.py — expose append(...)' — and the gate_audit_log DDL at design.md:1156-1167
change: The hash chain is strictly sequential (entry_hash = sha256(canonical_row || prev_hash) and INCLUDES the BIGSERIAL seq, per design.md:1166/1172), so two concurrent append() calls from different hook processes that read the same prev_hash but receive distinct seq values fork the chain at identical prev_hash — exactly the prev_hash != recomputed(prior) condition audit_verify treats as tampering (Property 28), a guaranteed false positive that bricks the merge gate. Add to task 52.1: append() MUST serialize prev_hash-read + insert under a lock (e.g. pg_advisory_xact_lock keyed on the log, or SELECT … FOR UPDATE on a chain-head sentinel) within one transaction so the chain linearizes. Mirror this as an Error-Handling note.
